body:
## Context
Part of the new note-taking app. Builds on the notes CRUD API and user authentication so a client can reconcile its local notes with the server.

## Goal
Provide a sync service that lets a client push local note changes and pull server changes since a given point, reconciling state per user.

## Scope
- A pull endpoint/function: given a since-cursor (timestamp or version), return notes created/updated/deleted after it for the current user.
- A push endpoint/function: accept a batch of client note changes and apply them, returning the reconciled result.
- A conflict-resolution strategy (e.g. last-write-wins by modified timestamp) decided during planning.
- Scoped per authenticated user.

## Acceptance criteria
- Pull with an old cursor returns the changes the client missed; pull with a current cursor returns nothing new.
- Push applies client changes and surfaces the canonical server state.
- Conflicts are resolved by the agreed deterministic strategy.
- Automated tests cover pull-since, push-apply, and a conflict case, runnable via `node --test`.

## Notes
Depends on the notes CRUD API and auth. Repo is a minimal CommonJS Node scaffold with a `node --test` harness. Third of three issues tracked in the GitHub Project.
title: Sync service

body:
## Context
Part of the new note-taking app. Builds on user authentication (issue: User authentication) so notes are scoped to and owned by the authenticated user.

## Goal
Provide a CRUD API for notes: create, read (single + list), update, and delete notes belonging to the authenticated user.

## Scope
- Create a note (e.g. title/body, owner = current user, timestamps).
- List the current user's notes; get a single note by id.
- Update a note's content.
- Delete a note.
- Enforce ownership: a user can only see/modify their own notes.

## Acceptance criteria
- Create returns a note with an id and owner.
- List returns only the requesting user's notes; get-by-id rejects access to another user's note.
- Update changes content and updates the modified timestamp.
- Delete removes the note; subsequent get returns not-found.
- Automated tests cover create/read/update/delete and the ownership boundary, runnable via `node --test`.

## Notes
Depends on the auth credential/verify helper from the authentication issue. Repo is a minimal CommonJS Node scaffold with a `node --test` harness. Second of three issues tracked in the GitHub Project.
title: Notes CRUD API

body:
## Context
Kicking off a new note-taking app. This is the foundational issue for user authentication, which the notes CRUD API and sync service both depend on for identifying and authorizing users.

## Goal
Provide a user authentication system: register a user, log them in, and issue/verify a session/token credential that downstream services (notes CRUD, sync) can use to identify the current user.

## Scope
- User registration (unique identifier such as email/username + password).
- Secure password handling (hashed, never stored in plaintext).
- Login that verifies credentials and returns a token/session.
- A way to verify a token and resolve it to a user (middleware/helper usable by other modules).

## Acceptance criteria
- A new user can be registered; duplicate registration is rejected.
- Login with correct credentials succeeds and yields a credential; wrong credentials are rejected.
- A helper exists to validate a credential and return the associated user (or reject).
- Passwords are stored hashed, not in plaintext.
- Automated tests cover register, login (success + failure), and token verification, runnable via `node --test`.

## Notes
The repo is a minimal CommonJS Node scaffold (src/, test/, `npm test` -> `node --test`). Keep the first cut in-memory/simple; no external DB required unless decided during planning. This is the first of three issues (auth, notes CRUD API, sync service) tracked together in a GitHub Project.
title: User authentication
